editionStmt.p: Digitized by the Ethiopic Manuscript Imaging Project directed by Steve Delamarter.

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

availability.licence: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

supportDesc.foliation:
The first foliation in pencil, probably by James Bruce, begins on the paper guard leaf. F. 94 has been numbered by Christian Friedrich August Dillmann as fol. 89. From this folio on, both foliations run until the end of the codex. In this description the second foliation by Christian Friedrich August Dillmann is used.
Le Liber Aksumae selon le manuscrit Bodleian Bruce 93 : le plus ancien témoin d'un projet historiographique sans cesse réactivéOriens ChristianusAnaisWion200993135-171http://www.academia.edu/3022230/Le_Liber_Aksumae_selon_le_manuscrit_Bodleian_Bruce_93_le_plus_ancien_t%C3%A9moin_dun_projet_historiographique_sans_cesse_r%C3%A9activ%C3%A9bm:Wion2009LibAksbm:Wion2009LibAks138101454214965

collation.note:
It is difficult to distinguish the original quires. Probably the sewing of the quires has been redone. Many false guard leaves, paper and parchment, have been added when the manuscript was rebound by James Bruce.
Le Liber Aksumae selon le manuscrit Bodleian Bruce 93 : le plus ancien témoin d'un projet historiographique sans cesse réactivéOriens ChristianusAnaisWion200993135-171http://www.academia.edu/3022230/Le_Liber_Aksumae_selon_le_manuscrit_Bodleian_Bruce_93_le_plus_ancien_t%C3%A9moin_dun_projet_historiographique_sans_cesse_r%C3%A9activ%C3%A9bm:Wion2009LibAksbm:Wion2009LibAks13810

binding.decoNote:
European binding; leather covering with gold leaf. The inner cover is reinforced with decorative paper. The French title is given on the spine by James Bruce in with golden letters. Many false paper and parchment guard leaves have been added. The edges were gilded, probably after the folios were recut.
Le Liber Aksumae selon le manuscrit Bodleian Bruce 93 : le plus ancien témoin d'un projet historiographique sans cesse réactivéOriens ChristianusAnaisWion200993135-171http://www.academia.edu/3022230/Le_Liber_Aksumae_selon_le_manuscrit_Bodleian_Bruce_93_le_plus_ancien_t%C3%A9moin_dun_projet_historiographique_sans_cesse_r%C3%A9activ%C3%A9bm:Wion2009LibAksbm:Wion2009LibAks13810

bibl.title: Catalogus codicum manuscriptorum Bibliothecae Bodleianae Oxoniensis, Pars VII: Codices Aethiopici
Le Liber Aksumae selon le manuscrit Bodleian Bruce 93 : le plus ancien témoin d'un projet historiographique sans cesse réactivé
Oriens Christianus
Travels to discover the source of the Nile, in the years 1768, 1769, 1770, 1771, 1772, and 1773, third edition
bibl.publisher: George Ramsay and Company for Archibald Constable and Company

support.note:
Very white and thin parchment.
Le Liber Aksumae selon le manuscrit Bodleian Bruce 93 : le plus ancien témoin d'un projet historiographique sans cesse réactivéOriens ChristianusAnaisWion200993135-171http://www.academia.edu/3022230/Le_Liber_Aksumae_selon_le_manuscrit_Bodleian_Bruce_93_le_plus_ancien_t%C3%A9moin_dun_projet_historiographique_sans_cesse_r%C3%A9activ%C3%A9bm:Wion2009LibAksbm:Wion2009LibAks14542

collation.note: These three leaves have been added at the beginning of the codex.

decoNote.desc:
Well executed ink drawing of a flying eagle holding a scroll with his beak and claws.
Le Liber Aksumae selon le manuscrit Bodleian Bruce 93 : le plus ancien témoin d'un projet historiographique sans cesse réactivéOriens ChristianusAnaisWion200993135-171http://www.academia.edu/3022230/Le_Liber_Aksumae_selon_le_manuscrit_Bodleian_Bruce_93_le_plus_ancien_t%C3%A9moin_dun_projet_historiographique_sans_cesse_r%C3%A9activ%C3%A9bm:Wion2009LibAksbm:Wion2009LibAks14542

item.q: From Ras Michael, Governor of the Province of Tigre, in Abyssinia.

msItem.title: ክብረ፡ ነገሥት።
msItem.incipit: በአኰቴቱ፡ ለእግዚአብሔር፡ አብ፡ አኃዜ፡ ኵሉ፡ ወበወልዱ፡ ኢየሱስ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ ዘቦቱ፡ ኵሉ፡ ኮነ፡ ዘእንበሌሁሰ፡ አልቦ፡ ዘኮነ። ወበመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ሥሉስ፡ ጰራቅሊጦስ፡ ዘይወፅእ፡ እምአብ፡ ወይነሥእ፡ እምወልድ፡ ፩ አምላክ፡ አብ፡ ወወልድ፡ ወመንፈስ፡ ቅዱስ፡ ነአምን፡ ወንገኒ፡ ለሥሉስ።
msItem.note: The titles of the 117 chapters are included in the text.

bibl.title: Kebra Nagast: Die Herrlichkeit der Könige, Nach den Handschriften in Berlin, London, Oxford und Paris, zum ersten Mal im äthiopischen Urtext herausgegeben und mit deutscher Übersetzung versehen von Carl Bezold
Abhandlungen der philosophisch-philologischen Klasse der Königlich Bayerischen Akademie der Wissenschaften
bibl.publisher: Verlag der K. B. Akademie der Wissenschaften in Kommission des G. Franz’schen Verlags (J. Roth)

series.title: Abhandlungen der philosophisch-philologischen Klasse der Königlich Bayerischen Akademie der Wissenschaften

msItem.title: Kǝbra nagaśt, ፩ ፡ በእንተ፡ ክብረ፡ ነገሥት፡
msItem.incipit: ፍካሬ፡ ወዜና፡ ዘ፫፻፲ወ፰፡ ርቱዓነ፡ ሃይማኖት፡ በእንተ፡ ክብር፡ ወዕበይ፡ ወተድላ፡ ዘከመ፡ ወሀበ፡ እግዚኣብሔር፡ ለደቂቀ፡ አዳም። ወፈድፋደሰ፡ ዘበእንተ፡ ዕበያ፡ ወክብራ፡ ለጽዮን፡ ታቦተ፡ ሕጉ፡ ለእግዚአብሔር፡ እንተ፡ ገባሪሃ፡ ወኬንያሃ፡ ለሊሁ፡ በውስተ፡ ጽርሐ፡ መቅደሱ፡ እምቅድመ፡ ኵሉ፡ ፍጥረት፡ ዘመላእክት፡ ወሰብእ፡

msItem.title: በእንተ፡ ዘለአከ፡ ንጉሥ፡ ሰሎሞን፡ መልአከ፡ ኀይሉ፡ ኀበ፡ ወልዱ፡
ኀበ፡ ተራከበ፡ ምስለ፡ ወልዱ፡ ንጉሥ፡ ሰሎሞን፡
ኀበ፡ መከረ፡ ንጉሥ፡ ይፈኑ፡ ወልዶ፡ ምስለ፡ ደቂቆሙ፡
ኀበ፡ አዘዞ፡ ሳዶቅ፡ ካህን፡ ለዳዊት፡ ንጉሥ፡
ኀበ፡ ተፋነዉ፡ ምስለ፡ አቡሁ፡ ወላሐወት፡ ሀገር፡
ኀበ፡ ዘይቤሎ፡ ለሳዶቅ፡ ካህን፡ ሑር፡ ወአምጽእ፡ ውእተ፡ ልብሰ፡ ዘላዕሌሃ፡
በእንተ፡ ግብአተ፡ ሳዶቅ፡ ካህን፡ ውሂቦ፡ ሞጻ፡
በእንተ፡ ቀዳሜ፡ ፍትሑ፡ ለአድራሚ፡ ንጉሠ፡ ሮም፡
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፸፯ ፡ በእንተ ፡ ንጉሠ ፡ ፋርስ ። ወንጉሠ ፡ ፋርስኒ ፡ ዘርአ ፡ ሴም ፡ ውእቱ ፡ ወዘበእንቲአሁ ፡ ናየድዐክሙ ።
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፸፰ ፡ በእንተ ፡ ንጉሠ ፡ ሞአብ ። ወንጉሠ ፡ ሞአብሂ ፡ ዘርአ ፡ ሴም ፡ ውእቱ ፡ ወናየድዐክሙ ፡ ዘከመሂ ፡ ኮነ ።
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፸፱ ፡ በእንተ ፡ ንጉሠ ፡ ዓማሌቅ ።
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፹ ፡ በእንተ ፡ ንጉሠ ፡ ኢሎፍሊ ። ወናሁ ፡ ኢሎፍሊ ፡ ዘርአ ፡ ሳምሶን ፡ ይነግሡ ፡ ላዕሌሆሙ ።
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፹፩ ፡ ዘከመ ፡ ቀተሎ ፡ ወልደ ፡ ሳምሶን ፡ ለወልደ ፡ ንጉሠ ፡ ኢሎፍሊ ።
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፹፪ ፡ በእንተ ፡ ርደተ ፡ አብርሃም ፡ ግብጽ ።
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፹፫ ፡ በእንተ ፡ ንጉሠ ፡ እስማኤላውያን ።
በእንተ፡ ንጉሠ፡ ኢትዮጵያ፡ ዘከመ፡ ገብአ፡ ሀገሩ፡
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፺፮ ፡ በእንተ ፡ ትንቢት ፡ ዘክርስቶስ ።
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፺፯ ፡ ኀበ ፡ አንጐርጐሩ ፡ እስራኤል ።
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፺፰ ፡ በእንተ ፡ በትረ ፡ ሙሴ ፡ ወአሮን ።
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፺፻ ፡ በእንተ ፡ ክልኤቱ ፡ አግብርት ።
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፻ ፡ በእንተ ፡ እለ ፡ ተዐደዉ ፡ መላእክት ።
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፻፩ ፡ በእንተ ፡ ህላዌ ፡ ኵለሄ ።
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፻፫ ፡ በእንተ ፡ አቅርንተ ፡ ምሥዋዕ ።
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፻፬ ፡ ካዕበ ፡ በእንተ ፡ ታቦት ፡ ወነገረ ፡ ዕልዋን ።
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፻፭ ፡ በእንተ ፡ አሚነ ፡ አብርሃም ።
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፻፮ ፡ ትንቢት ፡ በእንተ ፡ ምጽአቱ ፡ ለክርስቶስ ።
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፻፯ ፡ በእንተ ፡ በአቱ ፡ ኢየሩሳሌም ፡ በስብሐት ።
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፻፰ ፡ በእንተ ፡ እከየ ፡ ይሁዳ ፡ ዕልው ።
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፻፲፩ ፡ በእንተ ፡ ዕርገቱ ፡ ወዳግም ፡ ምጽአቱ ።
Kǝbra nagaśt, ፻፲፪ ፡ በእንተ ፡ ዘአርአዩ ፡ ነቢያት ፡ በሥጋሆሙ ።

msItem.note: The colophon, No item: LIT1709Kebran#Chapter118 which is usually included in manuscripts containing the Kǝbra nagaśt is absent in this manuscript. Le Liber Aksumae selon le manuscrit Bodleian Bruce 93 : le plus ancien témoin d'un projet historiographique sans cesse réactivéOriens ChristianusAnaisWion200993135-171http://www.academia.edu/3022230/Le_Liber_Aksumae_selon_le_manuscrit_Bodleian_Bruce_93_le_plus_ancien_t%C3%A9moin_dun_projet_historiographique_sans_cesse_r%C3%A9activ%C3%A9bm:Wion2009LibAksbm:Wion2009LibAks148

msItem.title: Documents on the History of ʾAksum
msItem.note: The description of the content of this manuscript follows Le Liber Aksumae selon le manuscrit Bodleian Bruce 93 : le plus ancien témoin d'un projet historiographique sans cesse réactivéOriens ChristianusAnaisWion200993135-171http://www.academia.edu/3022230/Le_Liber_Aksumae_selon_le_manuscrit_Bodleian_Bruce_93_le_plus_ancien_t%C3%A9moin_dun_projet_historiographique_sans_cesse_r%C3%A9activ%C3%A9bm:Wion2009LibAksbm:Wion2009LibAks.

msItem.title: Kama-zǝ nubāre śǝrʿātā la-ʾǝmmǝna Ṣǝyon gabaza ʾAksum
msItem.incipit: ከመዝ፡ ኑባሬ፡ ሥርዓታ፡ ለእምነ፡ ጽዮን፡ ገበዘ፡ አክሱም፡

msItem.title: Śǝrʿāta qʷǝrḥat
msItem.note: The text continues without intermission after , but the beginning of is rubricated.

bibl.title: Über die Regierung, insbesondere die Kirchenordnung des Königs Zarʾa-Jacob
bibl.publisher: Verlag der Königlichen Akademie der Wissenschaften

msItem.incipit: ዝውእቱ፡ መጽሐፍ፡ ዘወሀቦ፡ ሰሎሞን፡ ንጉሥ፡ ለዕብነ፡ ሐኪም፡ ወልዱ፡ ወወሀቦ፡ ሰብአ፡ ሕግ፡ እለ፡ የሐውሩ፡ ምስሌሁ፡ ሀገረ፡ ኢትዮጵያ።

msItem.title: Śǝrʿāta beta mangǝśt, List of twelve palace dignitaries, followed by a group of women, the "Daughters of Ṣǝyon", each listed with their attributes and objects representing their funtions.
Śǝrʿāta beta mangǝśt, List of the twelve houses of Israel which accompanied Mǝnilǝk I to Ethiopia.

bibl.title: Zur Geschichte des abyssinischen Reichs
Zeitschrift der deutschen morgenländischen Gesellschaft

msItem.title: Restauration of the royal throne in ʾAksum
Restauration of the royal throne in ʾAksum, Restauration of the throne of the kings of ʾAksum by Zarʾa Yāʿqob in the year 87
Restauration of the royal throne in ʾAksum, Restauration of the throne of the kings of ʾAksum by Śarḍa Dǝngǝl in the year 231
Land donation by Abraha and Aṣbǝḥa to ʾAksum Ṣǝyon
Rule for the protection of ʾAksum Ṣǝyon by Zarʾa Yāʿqob
List of the compulsory labours during the construction of ʾAksum Ṣǝyon
msItem.incipit: ከመዝአ፡ ጥንተ፡ ግብሮሙ፡ ለኵሎሙ፡ አህጉር፡ እለ፡ ይሰርሑ፡ ገበዘ፡ አክሱም።

msItem.title: History of the foundation of ʾAksum

msItem.title: Chronology of Umayyad and Abbasid califs, of the Fatimids and Ayyubites until 659 H.
ዝውእቱ፡ ዜና፡ ስሞሙ፡ ለአበዊነ፡ ሊቃነ፡ ጳጳሳት፡ ዘለእስክንድርያ፡
Maṣḥafa sǝddat
msItem.incipit: ወዓዲ፡ ንጽሕፍ፡ ለክሙ፡ ዳግማዌ፡ ታሪከ፡ በረድኤተ፡ እግዚእነ፡ ኢየሱስ፡ ክርስቶስ፡ መጽሐፈ፡ ስደቶሙ፡ ወዜና፡ ሞቶሙ፡ ለሕዝበ፡ ኢትዮጵያ፡ ወድምሳሴ፡ ኵሎሙ፡ አብያተ፡ ክርስቲያናት፡ ዘኮነ፡ በበዘመኖሙ፡

bibl.title: Le Liber Aksumae selon le manuscrit Bodleian Bruce 93 : le plus ancien témoin d'un projet historiographique sans cesse réactivé
Oriens Christianus

handNote.seg: Rubrication is used sparingly. In , the first two lines of both parts, as well as the name of Śarḍa Dǝngǝl and ማርያም፡ in the name of Baʾǝda Māryām I are rubricated.

item.desc: Pencil notes, mainly English translations of certain words, probably added by James Bruce or a collaborator, such as Alexander Murray.

history.provenance: This manuscript was produced at the order of Mikāʾel Sǝḥul and given by him to James Bruce.

collation.note: An empty leaf has been inserted between fols. 99, and 100.

projectDesc.p: Encoded according to TEI P5 Guidelines.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)
